title: Deliciously Shredded Venezuelan Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Venezuelan dish featuring shredded beef, pickle, and fresh ingredients, this empanada recipe is perfect for a meal prep snack or lunch, and is shojin-friendly, meaning it is vegetarian-friendly but contains animal by-products like beef, and can be adapted to suit various dietary needs.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat and cook the onion until it is translucent.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the shredded beef to the skillet and cook until it is heated through.
Stir in the pickle relish and season with salt and black pepper.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg and cut a small slit in the top of each empanada.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes, until golden brown.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
